reviews.review_msg: 3 hour lectures are mandatory, most classes are group work, bi-weekly quizzes. Assignments marked subjectively, deducts marks for incorrect citations. I would expect 0 for citation and it was a little surprising that they deduct marks that you've earned on other parts because citations has a minor mistake. Best to take 303 with other profs.

This is the hardest class I have taken at SFU so far. Grading is extremely unclear. Instructions for assignments are often hidden in lecture slides. All of the grading seems subjective and sometimes contradictory. While she is kind, she'll have you leaving every class frustrated and utterly confused. Choose another prof. Don't take this class.

She's passionate about this course... but her grading expectations are not what you expect and no one gets 100% on anything besides the quizzes. Hope that you get a good group because the averages for the group assignments are already super low.
You can tell she is passionate about the class through her lectures and the structure makes 3 hour lectures fly by

You are graded by many assignments and in-class work (no exams). Her feedback can be vague but she does outline what she expects, so you need to pay attention to what she says cause many don't. She doesn't post lecture slides which forced me to take notes (I found it helpful though). She seems to care for her students and will help you if you ask.

Would not recommend, not a good teacher, marking is unclear and seems to change with every assignment. Has in class exercises for practice gives hardly any feedback then marks way harder on actual activity.

90% of the work you do will be in class, such as quizzes, assignments, activities in your group that are made by random at the beginning of the semester. I liked it this way because it meant less homework and studying outside of class. Overall she cares about your learning and it's not hard to beat the curve if you do your readings and participate

Kathleens 303 course was frustrating due to subjective grading, unclear criteria, and inconsistent evaluation. Excessive readings and content left little room for meaningful engagement or reflection. Creative solutions were dismissed with comments like, “This isn't Disneyland,” discouraging critical thinking. Overall, the course felt discouraging.

Although Kathleen's lectures are engaging and the content is good, her grading and the overall work structure for her class is terrible. I've never seen a class where work is so subjectively graded. All her feedback along with her TA's is very opinionated, far from objective, and at times even contradictory.

Kathleen is a really good professor, she cares about you and gives you lots of practice before assessments. Her feedback is always detailed, and she explains her grading well. If you attend all classes and pay attention to her feedback, you will do well.

This class should be taken off from the reqs. It is literally bus272, just more group work. So repetitive, and purposeless. She was so boring but was quite unfair with marks. Did not highlight what she expected from you. I truly think that this class should not be paid for, and not worth the time.

I understand that mandatory attendance and heavy reliance on group projects may just not be for some people. But if you can handle it, take 303 with Kathleen. She is clearly very passionate on what she teaches, makes sure everyone gets more than enough feedback for every assignment. She gives you all the tools to succeed, you just gotta show up.

Kathleen's course is primarily in-class, with little homework and no slide postings. While she provides engaging lectures with group activities, her grading is tough and includes feedback on all submissions. However, her teaching style may not be suitable for international students or those who have difficulty focusing in class.

You can tell she likes teaching this course but slides should have been uploaded on canvas regardless. But many people won't come for this class if the slides are uploaded and this class has a A LOT of group work. Groups won't work without people so I understand why she doesn't upload the slides. Good prof, weird course. Ethics are imp but not fun
